name: Converter Build Workflow
description: Deterministic single-developer workflow that builds the SAME unit converter module as the converter-ad-hoc smoke test. The LLM only writes source/test code; the workflow engine performs all git commits and Jest runs deterministically via onExitCommands. This is the workflow-driven counterpart to converter-ad-hoc (LLM-driven) for A/B comparison.

CREATE_MODULE.name: Create Converter Module
CREATE_MODULE.description: Developer creates converter.ts with three conversion functions. The engine commits the file deterministically on exit.
CREATE_MODULE.prompt:
You are building a TypeScript unit converter module. The project is at {{projectPath}} and already has a Jest + TypeScript setup (package.json, jest.config.cjs, tsconfig.json) — do NOT create or modify those.

Your ONLY task in this step:

Create a file named `converter.ts` in {{projectPath}} that exports exactly these three functions:
  - `celsiusToFahrenheit(c: number): number` — returns (c * 9/5) + 32
  - `fahrenheitToCelsius(f: number): number` — returns (f - 32) * 5/9
  - `milesToKilometers(m: number): number` — returns m * 1.60934

Use `export function` declarations. Do not add any other functions yet.

IMPORTANT — the workflow engine handles process steps for you:
  - Do NOT run git or make commits.
  - Do NOT run jest or any tests.
  - Do NOT create test files yet.
Just write converter.ts and report what you created.

onExitCommands.reason: Gate: the developer must have created converter.ts before we can proceed.

onExitCommands.command: git add converter.ts && git commit -m 'feat: add unit converter module'
onExitCommands.reason: Deterministic incremental commit of the new module.

params.message: converter.ts created and committed for task {{taskId}}

WRITE_TESTS.name: Write Unit Tests
WRITE_TESTS.description: Developer writes converter.test.ts. The engine runs Jest (gating success), captures output, and commits deterministically.
WRITE_TESTS.prompt:
Continue building the converter module in {{projectPath}}. `converter.ts` already exists with celsiusToFahrenheit, fahrenheitToCelsius, and milesToKilometers.

Your ONLY task in this step:

Create a file named `converter.test.ts` in {{projectPath}} with Jest tests for the three existing functions. Include at least these 8 cases:
  - celsiusToFahrenheit(0) === 32
  - celsiusToFahrenheit(100) === 212
  - celsiusToFahrenheit(-40) === -40
  - fahrenheitToCelsius(32) === 0
  - fahrenheitToCelsius(212) === 100
  - fahrenheitToCelsius(-40) === -40
  - milesToKilometers(1) ≈ 1.60934 (use toBeCloseTo)
  - milesToKilometers(0) === 0

Import from './converter'. Use standard Jest `describe`/`it`/`expect`.

IMPORTANT — the workflow engine handles process steps for you:
  - Do NOT run git or make commits.
  - Do NOT run jest yourself — the engine runs the tests and will fail this step if they do not pass.
Just write converter.test.ts and report what you created.

onExitCommands.reason: Gate: the developer must have created converter.test.ts.
Run Jest deterministically: gates success (non-zero exit blocks the transition) AND captures the console output to test_output.txt.

onExitCommands.command: git add converter.test.ts && git commit -m 'test: add converter unit tests'
onExitCommands.reason: Deterministic incremental commit of the test file.

onExitCommands.command: git add test_output.txt && git commit -m 'docs: capture test output'
onExitCommands.reason: Deterministic commit of the captured Jest output.

params.message: Tests written, Jest passed, output captured for task {{taskId}}

UPDATE_README.name: Update README
UPDATE_README.description: Developer documents the converter in README.md. Runs after the module is complete so all four functions are documented. The engine commits deterministically.
UPDATE_README.prompt:
The converter module in {{projectPath}} is now complete: `converter.ts` exports all four functions (celsiusToFahrenheit, fahrenheitToCelsius, milesToKilometers, kilogramsToPounds) and the tests pass.

Your ONLY task in this step:

Update `README.md` in {{projectPath}} to describe the unit converter module. Include a short description and a usage example showing how to import and call EACH of the four functions, including kilogramsToPounds.

IMPORTANT — the workflow engine handles process steps for you:
  - Do NOT run git or make commits.
  - Do NOT run jest.
Just update README.md and report what you changed.

onExitCommands.command: git add README.md && (git diff --cached --quiet || git commit -m 'docs: update README with converter usage')
onExitCommands.reason: Deterministic commit of README changes, if any.

ADD_KG.name: Add kilogramsToPounds
ADD_KG.description: Developer extends converter.ts with a fourth function WITHOUT removing the existing three. The engine gates and commits deterministically.
ADD_KG.prompt:
Extend the existing converter module in {{projectPath}}. `converter.ts` already exports celsiusToFahrenheit, fahrenheitToCelsius, and milesToKilometers — you MUST keep all three.

Your ONLY task in this step:

Add one new exported function to `converter.ts`:
  - `kilogramsToPounds(kg: number): number` — returns kg * 2.20462

Do NOT remove or rename the existing functions. Do NOT modify the test file in this step.

IMPORTANT — the workflow engine handles process steps for you:
  - Do NOT run git or make commits.
  - Do NOT run jest.
Just edit converter.ts and report what you added.

onExitCommands.reason: Gate: converter.ts must now contain kilogramsToPounds.

onExitCommands.command: grep -q 'celsiusToFahrenheit' converter.ts && grep -q 'fahrenheitToCelsius' converter.ts && grep -q 'milesToKilometers' converter.ts
onExitCommands.reason: Gate: the original three functions must still be present (no overwrite).

onExitCommands.command: git add converter.ts && (git diff --cached --quiet || git commit -m 'feat: add kilogramsToPounds converter')
onExitCommands.reason: Deterministic commit of the extended module.

ADD_KG_TESTS.name: Test kilogramsToPounds
ADD_KG_TESTS.description: Developer adds tests for the fourth function. The engine re-runs Jest (gating success), recaptures output, and commits deterministically.
ADD_KG_TESTS.prompt:
Finish the converter module in {{projectPath}}. converter.ts now has four functions including kilogramsToPounds.

Your ONLY task in this step:

Add at least 3 new Jest test cases for kilogramsToPounds to the existing `converter.test.ts` (keep all existing tests). Include:
  - kilogramsToPounds(1) ≈ 2.20462 (use toBeCloseTo)
  - kilogramsToPounds(0) === 0
  - kilogramsToPounds(100) ≈ 220.462 (use toBeCloseTo)

The file must end up with at least 11 test cases total.

IMPORTANT — the workflow engine handles process steps for you:
  - Do NOT run git or make commits.
  - Do NOT run jest yourself — the engine runs the full test suite and will fail this step if any test does not pass.
Just edit converter.test.ts and report what you added.

onExitCommands.reason: Re-run the full Jest suite deterministically: gates success AND recaptures the updated output.

onExitCommands.command: git add converter.test.ts && (git diff --cached --quiet || git commit -m 'test: add kilogramsToPounds tests')
onExitCommands.reason: Deterministic commit of the new tests.

onExitCommands.command: git add test_output.txt && (git diff --cached --quiet || git commit -m 'docs: update test output with new tests')
onExitCommands.reason: Deterministic commit of the refreshed Jest output.

params.message: kilogramsToPounds tests added, full suite passed for task {{taskId}}

DONE.name: Complete
DONE.description: Terminal state. The converter module is built, tested, documented, and committed incrementally.
